page: Microsoft Azure E16-8as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2702.79, placing it in the top tier of tested instances. Multi-core CPU performance and memory read bandwidths are average, with small-block cached read bandwidth reaching 516.37 GB/sec and large-block uncached read bandwidth at 39.4 GB/sec. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in large-block uncached memory write bandwidth, which is weak at 22.29 GB/sec. Similarly, while LLM text generation with a medium 7B model is average at 9.34 tokens/sec, prompt processing for the same model is weak at 17.33 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16-8as_v5 offers high memory density relative to its compute allocation, allowing memory-bound workloads to run without paying for unneeded vCPUs. This resource profile is suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-8as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores. The instance features 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, alongside average multi-core and memory read speeds. However, large-block memory write bandwidth and medium-model LLM prompt processing represent performance tradeoffs, falling into the lower tier. This server is structurally suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching, and enterprise applications that benefit from high memory density relative to compute capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 8 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor and 128 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, designed for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and dedicated AMD processors for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Tradeoffs in large-block memory write bandwidth and medium LLM prompt processing
No local storage or GPU resources included
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy enterprise applications
